explanation: The LTL property states that globally (G), if the power button is pressed (bool_exp1) and all specified conditions regarding system connections (breathing circuit, no patient, air supply, and power source) are met (bool_exp2), then the Start-up Mode (bool_exp3) will be active in the very next state (X). This captures the 'initiated by' aspect, implying that the Start-up Mode begins immediately after the combined trigger conditions are met.

The natural language statement specifies that the "Start-up Mode" (bool_exp3) should be initiated. The initiation is triggered by "pushing the power button" (part of bool_exp2) and requires several preconditions to be met: the system must be connected to the breathing circuit, the air supply, and the power source, and specifically "without connection to the patient" (all forming bool_exp2). The phrase "shall be initiated" suggests that this mode will become active in the next state after the conditions are met. Template 11, 'G (bool_exp2 -> (X bool_exp3))', perfectly captures this, stating that globally, if the combined conditions in bool_exp2 are met, then bool_exp3 will be true in the next state.

This LTL formula captures a global response property. It states that whenever the power button is pressed AND the system is connected to the breathing circuit, air supply, and power source (while NOT connected to the patient), then in the very next time step, the system must enter the Start-up Mode. This accurately reflects the initiation condition as a direct consequence of the specified trigger event and preconditions.

The LTL property states that globally (G), if the system is connected to the breathing circuit (bool_exp1: `breathingCircuitConnected`), the air supply (`airSupplyConnected`), and the power source (bool_exp1: `powerConnected`, interpreting this atomic proposition as connection to the power source given the natural language context despite its ambiguous description of being connected to the breathing circuit), AND the power button is pushed (bool_exp2: `powerButton`), then the Start-up Mode (bool_exp3: `StartUpMode`) shall be initiated in the next state (X bool_exp3). This precisely captures the temporal relationship described: the initiation (next state) is a direct consequence of the specified conditions and trigger occurring simultaneously. The phrase "without connection to the patient" is implicitly satisfied by not including `patientConnected` in the antecedent.

The property states that globally, if the power button is pressed AND the system has been connected to the breathing circuit, air supply, and power source, then in the very next state, the Start-up Mode will be initiated. This directly captures the requirement that the initiation is triggered by the power button press, but only when the specified pre-conditions regarding connections are met. The "once" in the prompt implies that the conditions must be true at the moment of the trigger, and "initiated by pushing" indicates a direct, immediate (next-state) consequence.

The property states that globally, whenever the power button is pressed and the system is connected to the breathing circuit (without a patient), the air supply, and the power source, the Start-up Mode must be initiated. This captures the conditional initiation of the Start-up Mode, ensuring it only begins when all specified connections are established and the power button is activated.

The property states that the 'Start-up Mode' is initiated when a specific set of conditions are met: the 'power button' is pushed, the system is 'breathingCircuitConnected', 'airSupplyConnected', 'powerConnected' (interpreted as connected to power source, as per natural language context), and 'patientConnected' is false. This is captured by using template 60, where bool_exp1 represents this combined trigger condition, and bool_exp3 represents 'StartUpMode'. The template ensures that if this combined trigger condition becomes true (i.e., it was false in the previous state and is true now), then 'StartUpMode' will be true in the very next state. Additionally, if this combined condition is true at the initial state (time 0), 'StartUpMode' must also be true at time 0. This accurately translates 'shall be initiated by pushing... once...'.

The property states that globally, if the power button is pressed while the system is connected to the breathing circuit (without a patient), the air supply, and the power source, then the Start-up Mode will be active in the very next state. This captures the initiation of the mode upon the specified conditions and action.

The property states that globally, if the system's power button is pushed while it is connected to the breathing circuit (without a patient), air supply, and power source, then the system must enter Start-up Mode. This captures the immediate initiation of the Start-up Mode upon fulfilling all specified conditions and pressing the power button.

The LTL property states that globally, if the power button is pushed AND the system is connected to the breathing circuit (without a patient) AND the air supply AND the power source, then the Start-up Mode will be initiated in the next state. This captures the requirement for the Start-up Mode to be initiated by specific trigger conditions and preconditions.
